articles.title: THE AI ERA REQUIRES A DIFFERENT KIND OF EXPERIMENTATION
articles.summary: Product experimentation must evolve in the AI era, as traditional assumptions no longer apply. Shifts include collapsing UI surfaces, faster development cycles, diminished value in minor copy tweaks due to AI personalization, and increased inference costs that raise the stakes for monetization experiments.

articles.title: HOW TO MEASURE DEVELOPMENT PRODUCTIVITY?
articles.summary: Developer activity metrics like code, commits, tokens, and features measure output, not value. Measure cross-functional teams by the business and user outcomes they create instead.

articles.title: WHAT BEING "INSPIRING" ACTUALLY MEANS
articles.summary: Inspiring at work means presenting a clear point of view and showing people, concretely, why taking action will make their work better.

articles.title: THE NEW INNER GAME
articles.summary: As AI makes knowledge and execution abundant, emotional clarity becomes a critical advantage. The people and teams that thrive will make discerning decisions, confront conflict, embrace failure, and manage fear without letting it dictate their actions.

articles.title: IS AI FOOLING YOU?
articles.summary: AI accelerates execution, but without shared vision and collaboration, it only magnifies misalignment. Strong product teams use AI to speed up iteration while keeping humans focused on judgment, alignment, and solving problems that matter.

articles.title: STOP WRITING BLOG POSTS AND CALLING IT SEO
articles.summary: Publishing blog posts for SEO is often just content marketing masquerading as SEO, which frequently fails to drive business outcomes. A true product-led SEO strategy prioritizes making your product discoverable at the moment of intent, routing users directly into action-oriented conversion flows rather than passive reading.

articles.title: BUILD THE AGENT OR POWER THE AGENT?
articles.summary: Founders must choose between "building the agent" and "powering the agent”. This decision hinges on where the customer works, whether the company owns the full workflow or a slice, and where the product's value resides: in data/context or capabilities/actions.

articles.title: RICK AND MORTY'S STORYTELLING SECRET TO HOOK ANY READER
articles.summary: Dan Harmon's Story Circle turns the Hero's Journey into eight repeatable steps that move a character from comfort through conflict, transformation, and consequences. Its strict structure creates room for creativity, helping writers turn absurd ideas into accessible stories rooted in meaningful human struggles.

articles.title: QUARTERLY PLANNING IS A SCARCITY RITUAL
articles.summary: Quarterly planning is an outdated 'scarcity ritual.'

articles.title: PRODUCT MANAGER IS THE OLD TITLE
articles.summary: As AI reduces shipping costs, the traditional PM role is shifting toward Complexity Management: prioritizing strategic restraint, value clarity, ecosystem-level thinking, and the judgment to recalibrate.
